name: esp-decoder
description: Decode ESP32 / ESP8266 crash dumps from serial port

commands.title: ESP Decoder: Open Serial Monitor & Crash Decoder
ESP Decoder: Copy MCP Server URL

esp-decoder.defaultBaudRate.description: Default baud rate for serial connections. Common values: 9600, 19200, 38400, 57600, 74880, 115200, 230400, 460800, 921600. Custom (non-standard) baud rates are also accepted.

esp-decoder.autoDetectElf.description: Automatically detect ELF file from PlatformIO or ESP-IDF build directories

esp-decoder.elfPath.description: Manual path to ELF file (overrides auto-detection)

esp-decoder.toolPath.description: Manual path to GDB/addr2line tool (overrides auto-detection)

esp-decoder.targetArch.description: Target architecture for decoding (auto will try to detect from ELF/board)

esp-decoder.serialMonitor.maxLines.description: Maximum number of lines to keep in serial monitor

esp-decoder.serialMonitor.autoscroll.description: Automatically scroll to bottom on new serial data

esp-decoder.serialMonitor.autoReconnect.description: Automatically reconnect to the serial port if it disappears (e.g. during reset on boards with native USB-CDC such as ESP32-S2/S3/C3). Reconnection only occurs when a port reappears with the same VID/PID/serialNumber as the originally connected device.

esp-decoder.serialMonitor.autoReconnectT.description: Maximum time (in milliseconds) to keep polling for the device to reappear before giving up auto-reconnect.

esp-decoder.logDirectory.description: Directory where Log2File output is written. Relative paths are resolved from the first workspace folder.

enumDescriptions: Open monitor in bottom panel (next to Terminal, Output, etc.)
Open monitor as editor tab (legacy behavior)
esp-decoder.monitorLocation.description: Location where the ESP Crash Monitor should open

esp-decoder.serialFilters.timestamp.description: Prepend a timestamp (HH:MM:SS.mmm) to each line in the serial monitor

esp-decoder.serialFilters.suppressPatter.description: Regex pattern — lines matching this pattern are hidden in the serial monitor (e.g. '^\s*$' to hide blank lines)

esp-decoder.serialFilters.highlightPatte.description: Regex pattern — matches are highlighted (inverse colours) in the serial monitor

esp-decoder.serialFilters.dedupPattern.description: Regex pattern for repeated characters/strings to collapse in the serial monitor (e.g. \\. for dots). Matches beyond the threshold are hidden and counted.

esp-decoder.serialFilters.dedupThreshold.description: Number of regex matches to show before collapsing repeated characters (dedupPattern)

esp-decoder.debugLogging.description: Emit verbose diagnostic messages (e.g. internal state of the serial-port manager, auto-reconnect classification) to the ESP Decoder output channel. Enable when troubleshooting; leave off for normal use.

esp-decoder.mcp.enabled.description: Run an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server on localhost so AI agents (e.g. Claude Code) can drive the serial monitor: connect/disconnect, read the log, upload firmware, and decode crashes. The endpoint is unauthenticated and bound to 127.0.0.1 only.

esp-decoder.mcp.port.description: TCP port for the MCP server (streamable HTTP at http://127.0.0.1:<port>/mcp). Use a per-workspace override when running multiple VS Code windows.

esp-decoder.improv.timeout.description: Timeout (ms) for each Improv-WiFi serial command (device info, network scan, provisioning). Applies to the WiFi provisioning button and the provision_wifi MCP tool.
